task: Extract the company and the buying intent from an inbound sales email
note: Deterministic checks fit routine, high-volume steps (extraction, classification, formatting) - the steps where model routing saves the most. Judgment-heavy steps stay on the frontier model and are not what this harness is for. Replace these with cases from your own task; ask Aletheia to write a set for you. The company checks use a case-insensitive regex (?i) because models echo the lowercased email domain (for acme.io they return company=acme). Use regex (?i) or iequals for free-form extracted values, and plain contains for controlled enums like the intent field.

cases.name: clear-demo-request
cases.system: Extract two fields from the email. Reply with only: company=<name>; intent=<one of: demo, pricing, support, other>
cases.prompt:
From: jane@acme.io
Subject: next steps
Hi, we saw your platform and would like to book a demo next week for our ops team.

cases.name: pricing-question
cases.system: Extract two fields from the email. Reply with only: company=<name>; intent=<one of: demo, pricing, support, other>
cases.prompt:
From: ops@northwind.co
Subject: cost
What does your enterprise plan run for 200 seats?
